judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports independently discover the same prior Edgerunner attempt/revert via git history and hit all four expectations with specific commit citations; Report 2 is slightly more thorough (e.g., explicitly notes the incident is unaddressed since the revert and offers a lower-risk cost alternative), but both are comparable in substance.

report_1.evidence: `git log --oneline -i --grep="revert"` in `carelog-api` — this alone surfaces that the exact migration was already attempted and reverted (commit `1712565`).

report_2.evidence: Ticket PLAT-31: Edgerunner was added (`454a21f`), the per-request unpooled connection fix was shipped as a stopgap (`597777b`), and it was fully reverted in production (`1712565`)

report_1.evidence: patched to open a dedicated Postgres connection per request (`597777b`) as a stopgap because Edgerunner isolates can't hold the shared pool, and that stopgap itself caused `/reports/monthly` to exhaust Postgres `max_connections` in production, taking the endpoint down — at which point the team reverted back to Cargobox.

report_2.evidence: it was fully reverted in production (`1712565`) because it "exhausted Postgres `max_connections`" and took `/reports/monthly` down with `FATAL: too many connections`.

report_1.evidence: steps (a) and (b) are not hypothetical; they were already built and are recoverable from git history at `454a21f` (`backend/serverless_handler.py`, `deploy/edgerunner.toml`), so this would be a second attempt, not a first...at which point the team reverted back to Cargobox.

report_2.evidence: This exact migration was already tried and failed in this repo. Ticket PLAT-31: Edgerunner was added (`454a21f`)... and it was fully reverted in production (`1712565`)

report_1.evidence: this is a call for whoever owns `carelog-api`/`PLAT` tickets (the ticket scheme suggests a "Platform" team/owner tracking issues as `PLAT-N`; `PLAT-31` is the specific ticket for this history), not something to silently re-attempt.

report_2.evidence: Whoever owns ticket PLAT-31 / has the incident context should be consulted, since they'll know what was tried after the revert (if anything) and why it hasn't been re-attempted since April.
